name: disk_asset_load
title: Game asset loading
short_description: Streaming and unpacking a bundled game asset package from storage.

structured_text.description: Loads a bundled package of game assets from storage and unpacks it into usable form, mixing large sequential reads of packed data with the many smaller reads and decompression steps a game performs while loading a level. The package is fixed so the result reflects how a real load screen behaves.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the number of asset bundles loaded and unpacked per minute, reported as items per minute.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. A higher rate means shorter loading screens and less stutter when a game streams new areas.
structured_text.reasoning: Game loading combines reading, seeking and decompression in one workload, which represents storage in real use far better than any single access pattern.

domain_gaming.explanation: Level loading and world streaming are exactly this workload.

domain_office.explanation: Opening large bundled files shares the read and unpack pattern.

domain_software_development.explanation: Unpacking large archives and dependency caches mirrors this work.
